<!-- Generated by ArDD's install.sh — overwritten on every install/update.
     Do not hand-edit; changes belong in the ArDD source repo's
     templates/dot-project-readme.md. -->

# How to read `.project/`

This directory is the state of an [artifact-driven-dev
(ArDD)](https://github.com/tylerpeckenpaugh/artifact-driven-dev) workflow:
declared artifacts refined iteratively, plus the plans, tasks, and reports
the skills generate from them. If you are reviewing this repository —
human or AI — this note tells you which files mean what, and which ones
only *look* live.

## Authored, living documents

- `artifacts/*.md` — the system's declared state (`constitution.md`
  nearly always, plus domain artifacts as the project warrants). These
  are the source of truth the whole workflow plans against.
  `status: draft` vs `status: stable` frontmatter gates whether an
  artifact is safe to plan against.
- `features/*.md` — the per-feature register. Each file's `status`
  frontmatter (`backlogged → planned → tasked → implemented`, or
  `rejected`/`subsumed`/`retired`) is live lifecycle state.
- `feedback/*.md` — captured observations. Open items (`- [ ]`) are
  live; a file whose frontmatter says `status: planned` is a **static
  historical record** — its items were consumed by a plan and are not
  re-triaged.

## Static historical records (they look live, but aren't)

- `plans/plan-*.md` — a plan is a snapshot of intent at drafting time.
  Its phase lists are plain enumerations of plan work-items, **not**
  checklists; nothing ever ticks a plan, and `status: superseded` or an
  old date does not mean abandoned work. Progress lives only in the
  linked tasks file.
- `tasks/tasks-*.md` with `status: completed` (or `abandoned`) — a
  finished (or superseded) execution record. Only a `ready`/`in-progress`
  tasks file's checkboxes are live, and they are ticked exclusively by
  `/ardd-implement`.
- `research/`-style one-off outputs in `plans/` — investigation results
  with no lifecycle.

## Correcting a skill-written file

**Factual corrections are exempt from the no-hand-edit rule.** A factual
correction fixes content that is *wrong on the page* without re-deciding
anything: a mis-cited file or symbol, a stale path, a typo, a wrong
quotation. Anyone (human or AI) may hand-edit these in place, in any
skill-written file's body prose. The exemption never covers frontmatter
`status` fields, checkboxes, or any lifecycle state — those stay
script-mutated (`ardd-state.sh`) — and never covers decisions, scope, or
classifications: changing *what was decided* goes through the workflow
(`/ardd-feedback`, `/ardd-refine`, a new plan).

## Generated reports (single-writer, disposable)

`STATUS.md` (written only by `/ardd-status`), `DEFECTS.md`
(`/ardd-defects`), `TRACKER.md` (`/ardd-tracker`), and `audit.md`
(`/ardd-audit`) are regenerated wholesale from disk by their one owning
skill. At merge/rebase they are disposable — either side may be taken
without deliberation and the owner regenerates. Do not review their diffs
as if they were hand-maintained; staleness here means "owner hasn't rerun
yet", not neglect.

`ardd-version.md` is the committed record of which ArDD version produced
the installed skills. This `README.md` itself is generated and
overwritten by `install.sh`.

## Elsewhere in the repo

`.claude/skills/` is regenerated output of ArDD's `install.sh` and is
normally gitignored (`.claude/skills/ardd-*/`) — never review it as
project source. The prose conventions above (single-writer ownership,
static-record semantics) are enforced by convention and lint
(`/ardd-lint`), not by hooks.
